summary: Finalized the Harisen item decomp by adding its item state table, changing one dropped-state helper call, cleaning up related GALE01 symbol scopes, and flipping src/melee/it/items/itharisen.c from NonMatching to Matching in configure.py. The PR has no body or review discussion, so the rationale is inferred from the title and diff.

key_files.reason: Added ItemStateTable it_803F5FF0 and changed itHarisen_Logic24_Dropped to call Item_8026849C instead of it_8026B390.
Converted several itharisen-adjacent sdata/sdata2 symbols from global it_804D... labels to local @NNN labels while preserving sizes, alignments, and data kinds.

what_changed: Added ItemStateTable it_803F5FF0[] with ten Harisen motion entries using itHarisen_UnkMotion*_Anim, *_Phys, and *_Coll callbacks.
The new state table includes animation IDs -1, 0, 1, 2, 3, and 4, plus NULL collision callbacks for several UnkMotion6 entries.
In itHarisen_Logic24_Dropped, replaced it_8026B390(gobj) with Item_8026849C(gobj) before Item_80268E5C(gobj, 8, 6).
Marked melee/it/items/itharisen.c as Matching in configure.py.
Changed two .sdata string labels at 0x804D5230 and 0x804D5238 from global it_804D5230/it_804D5238 to local @254/@255.
Changed multiple .sdata2 float/double labels from global it_804DC900 through it_804DC960-style names to local @168, @169, @170, @171, @253, @256, @257, @321, @322, @412, and @471-@476 labels.
Changed .sdata2 labels at 0x804DCAF8 and 0x804DCAFC from global it_804DCAF8/it_804DCAFC to local @154/@171.
smart_moves: Completed the source, build configuration, and symbol-map pieces together before marking the object Matching, avoiding a source-only match claim.
Restored the item state table as C data instead of leaving the motion dispatch data implicit or symbol-only.
Preserved exact symbol metadata for literals, including object size, alignment, and float/double/string data type, while correcting scope from global to local.
Used the alternate existing helper Item_8026849C in dropped logic, which the diff implies was needed for the final match; no PR text explains the semantic distinction from it_8026B390.
decomp_lessons: When finalizing an item file, update configure.py, source data declarations, and config/GALE01/symbols.txt in the same PR.
ItemStateTable reconstruction is often a matching blocker for item files; include the exact callback ordering of anim, phys, coll and preserve NULL entries.
Temporary global labels like it_804Dxxxx in symbols.txt may need to become local @NNN labels once function-local literals are understood.
Matching can hinge on selecting the correct existing item helper call even when helper names are still address-like or only partially understood.
If no PR discussion exists, treat details such as why Item_8026849C matched better than it_8026B390 as unproven unless later comments or code explain it.
naming_conventions: Harisen item callbacks use the itHarisen_ prefix, including itHarisen_Logic24_Dropped and itHarisen_UnkMotion*_Anim/Phys/Coll.
The item state table remained address-named as it_803F5FF0 rather than receiving a semantic name in this PR.
Local compiler literals in symbols.txt use @NNN labels instead of exported it_804D... labels.
The changed function keeps the common item local naming style: Item_GObj* gobj and Item* ip.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Declare the ItemStateTable in C with the exact sequence of Harisen motion callbacks and repeated UnkMotion6/UnkMotion8 entries.
Use NULL collision callback slots where the table requires no collision function; do not substitute empty functions.
For itHarisen_Logic24_Dropped, the matching call sequence after resetting scale is Item_8026849C(gobj) followed by Item_80268E5C(gobj, 8, 6).
For small data literals, keep addresses, sizes, alignments, and float/double widths stable while changing only symbol scope/name.
review_feedback: No PR body, issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the slice; there is no explicit reviewer feedback to extract.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename it_803F5FF0 or resolve the itHarisen_UnkMotion* callback names.
Compare Item_8026849C and it_8026B390 usage in other item drop/reset paths.
Search for other item finalization PRs that pair ItemStateTable reconstruction with symbols.txt local @NNN cleanup.
Check whether later review or code comments explain the semantic difference behind the Item_8026849C call in itHarisen_Logic24_Dropped.
